_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize GA state-legislature batch: trey-kelley (HD16 R), gail-davenport (SD17 D), martin-momtahan (HD17 R), steven-mcneel (SD18 R freshman), tyler-smith (HD18 R). All five web-verified as current incumbents seeking 2026 reelection. Scored from sponsorships, Freedom Index roll calls, party-line marquee votes, issue-group ratings/endorsements, and cited campaign statements. Null where no evidence; never inferred from party.

profile.background: Attorney (Cedartown); GA House District 16 since 2013. Former House Majority Whip (resigned 2021); now Vice Chair of Health and of Ways & Means.

trey-kelley@GA.notes_set: Web-verified current HD-16 incumbent (R) seeking 2026 reelection (Ballotpedia; legis.ga.gov). Self-stated record on campaign site (kelleyforhouse.com) lists Heartbeat Bill, Constitutional Carry, anti-CRT legislation, and Parent's Bill of Rights among accomplishments -> scored those cells TRUE. Most roll-call-level and interest-group-rating cells returned no retrievable evidence this pass -> nulled (not fabricated). Leadership descriptor corrected: he is a FORMER Majority Whip (resigned 2021), not current.

0.note: Campaign site lists the Heartbeat Bill among his pro-life record; self-identified pro-life.

1.note: Campaign site lists Parent's Bill of Rights among his accomplishments (parental rights).

2.note: Campaign site lists anti-Critical Race Theory legislation among his accomplishments.

0.note: Campaign site lists Constitutional Carry (GA SB319, 2022) among his accomplishments.

profile.background: Real-estate professional and longtime civil-rights activist; GA Senate 2007-09 and 2011-present (SD-44 through 2025, redistricted to SD-17 for 2025).

gail-davenport@GA.notes_set: Web-verified current SD-17 incumbent (D) seeking 2026 reelection (Ballotpedia). Documented progressive record. HARD: co-sponsored SB19 (2025), a 10-day firearm waiting period (Brady-law) -> pro-gun-control. Party-line marquee votes where Senate Democrats uniformly opposed (she was a sitting Democrat for each): HB481 heartbeat (2019), SB202 election law (2021), SB319 constitutional carry (2022), SB233 vouchers (2024), SB63 cash-bail (2024) -> scored the relevant cells FALSE per the recorded party-line side. No conservative-side deviation found. Remaining cells nulled.

0.note: HB481 heartbeat bill (2019) passed Senate 34-18 party-line, Democrats opposed; she was a sitting Senate Democrat -> pro-choice.

1.note: Opposed even heartbeat restrictions; no support for abolition.

0.note: SB233 (2024) school-voucher bill passed Senate 33-21 party-line, Democrats opposed -> against school choice.

1.note: SB202 (2021 Election Integrity Act, incl. ID for absentee) passed on party lines, Senate Democrats opposed.
SB63 (2024) cash-bail expansion passed 30-17, Democrats opposed -> against tougher bail.

0.note: SB319 (2022) constitutional carry passed 34-22 party-line, Democrats opposed.

1.note: Co-sponsored SB19 (2025), a 10-day firearm waiting period (Brady-law) -> supports gun control.

profile.background: Small-business owner (driving school, Paulding County); GA House District 17 since 2019.

martin-momtahan@GA.notes_set: Web-verified current HD-17 incumbent (R) seeking 2026 reelection (Ballotpedia; legis.ga.gov). Documented conservative: co-sponsor HB441 (2025) Prenatal Equal Protection Act (personhood from fertilization, backed by GA Right to Life); YES SB140 (2023, trans-minors ban); YES HB1178 (2022, Parents' Bill of Rights); primary sponsor HB215 (2025, ERIC withdrawal / voter-data); YES SB345 (2022, vaccine-passport ban); sponsor HB1102 (anti-sanctuary inmate transfer); YES SB319 (2022, constitutional carry) + sponsor HB903 (2A protection). Freedom Index 'deviations' are free-speech framing artifacts, not left votes -> not scored as non-conservative. No real conservative-side deviation. Economic-stewardship cells (CBDC/balanced-budget/ESG) had no specific evidence -> nulled.

0.note: Co-sponsor HB441 (2025) Prenatal Equal Protection Act (personhood from fertilization); campaign: 'Life begins at conception.'

1.note: HB441 is an abortion-abolition (equal-protection) bill, not merely a restriction.

2.note: Voted YES on SB140 (2023) banning gender-transition procedures for minors -> affirms biological sex.

1.note: Voted YES on HB1178 (2022) Parents' Bill of Rights.

0.note: Campaign: backs 'the right for people to pray, worship, and exercise their religion without interference.'

4.note: Primary sponsor HB215 (2025) Voter Integrity Act: withdraw GA from ERIC and bar sharing voter data with non-governmental entities.

1.note: Voted YES on SB345 (2022) vaccine-passport ban (anti-mandate).

2.note: Sponsor HB1102 directing monthly listing of near-release illegal-alien inmates for transfer to sanctuary states (anti-sanctuary).

0.note: Voted YES on SB319 (2022) constitutional carry.

1.note: Sponsor of HB903 Second Amendment Restoration/Protection firearms bill.

profile.background: Attorney (Macon); won Feb 2026 special runoff for GA Senate District 18 (seat vacated by John Kennedy's Lt-Gov run); sworn in Feb 25, 2026.

steven-mcneel@GA.notes_set: Web-verified: won Feb 17, 2026 special runoff, sworn in Feb 25, 2026 (Ballotpedia; Capitol Beat). FRESHMAN with effectively no roll-call record yet. Scored only from clear, cited campaign-stated positions: protecting the unborn, supporting school choice, defending religious liberty, backing law enforcement. Vague slogans ('cut taxes', generic '2A rights', 'deport criminal illegal aliens') did not meet a specific rubric cell's bar -> nulled. Re-research after the 2027 session yields a voting record. Note: plaintiff's trial lawyer / GTLA member (watch tort-reform votes) -- no vote yet, not scored.

0.note: Campaign: 'voting to protect the unborn'; 'Protecting life' listed as a priority.
Campaign: 'Put parents and children first by supporting school choice.'
Campaign announcement lists 'Defending religious liberty' as a priority.
Campaign: 'Back the Blue and give our law enforcement the funds needed' -> affirmative pro-police statement.

profile.background: Attorney (Bremen); GA House District 18 since 2021; Chairman, House Judiciary Non-Civil Committee.

tyler-smith@GA.notes_set: Web-verified current HD-18 incumbent (R, Bremen) seeking 2026 reelection (Ballotpedia; legis.ga.gov member 4990 -- correct Tyler Smith). Strongly socially/religiously conservative: lead sponsor HB496 (2023, abortion=homicide from fertilization); GA Life Alliance certification + GA Right to Life endorsement (2020, no exceptions); NO on HB428 (2025 'Right to IVF'); YES SB140 (trans-minors ban) and HB1084 (Protect Students First); YES HB1178 (Parents' Bill of Rights); House sponsor + YES on SB36 (2025 GA RFRA); YES SB442 (2026 immigration/driver's-license-status); YES SB319 (constitutional carry). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: voted NO on SB233 (2023) school vouchers, joining ~16 rural Republicans who sank it -> family q0 FALSE. Elections/bail/10A/NRA cells genuinely thin -> nulled.

0.note: Lead sponsor HB496 (2023) defining abortion from fertilization as homicide; GA Life Alliance certification + GA Right to Life endorsement (2020, no exceptions).

1.note: HB496 is an abolition bill (abortion = homicide), not merely a restriction.

2.note: Voted NO on HB428 (2025) 'Right to IVF' -> opposes IVF-embryo protections.
Voted YES on SB140 (2023) banning gender-transition procedures for minors.

4.note: Voted YES on HB1084 (2022) Protect Students First Act (transgender sports/curriculum).

0.note: DEVIATION: voted NO on SB233 (2023) Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers, joining rural Republicans who defeated it 85-89 -> against universal school choice.

1.note: Voted YES on HB1178 (2022) Parents' Bill of Rights.

2.note: Voted YES on HB1084 (2022) Protect Students First Act (curriculum/SOGI).

0.note: House sponsor of GA RFRA (SB36, 2025); voted YES on House passage.

1.note: RFRA provides conscience-exemption protections he championed.

3.note: Voted YES on SB442 (2026) tying driver's-license eligibility to immigration/visa status (status verification for a state credential).

0.note: Voted YES on SB319 (2022) constitutional carry.
